alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years scaling global commercial operations at Google Devices and Services with experience in pricing, revenue, and cross-functional commercial decision-making
alignments.job_requirement: Commercial Steering: support pricing, revenue management, sales, and marketing levers to achieve targets

alignments.candidate_evidence: Demonstrated ability to drive revenue-focused transformation programs and deliver data-driven insights; comfortable with analytics and stakeholder influence
alignments.job_requirement: Performance & Insights: track demand, competition, and KPIs; deliver clear, data-driven recommendations to improve revenue

alignments.candidate_evidence: Leadership on commercial operations scaling suggests strategy input and competitive positioning experience; direct region-level execution experience is unstated
alignments.job_requirement: Regional Commercial Strategy: maintain competitive positioning insights, support planning cycles with market briefings

alignments.candidate_evidence: Proven cross-functional stakeholder management across multiple teams at Google; transformation program leadership requires broad coalition building
alignments.job_requirement: Cross-Functional Collaboration: align with Revenue Management, Marketing, Sales, Network Planning, and Product teams

alignments.candidate_evidence: Experienced in analytics and data-driven decision-making; SQL experience not clearly stated; Excel and BI tool proficiency not confirmed in profile
alignments.job_requirement: Analytics & Stakeholder Support: use analytics and tools (Excel, BI, SQL) to inform decisions

gaps.gap: Direct Revenue Management or Pricing expertise: JD emphasizes revenue management and pricing as core levers; candidate profile focuses on commercial operations and transformation rather than deep pricing strategy or yield management
gaps.mitigation: Revenue management and pricing fundamentals are learnable and candidate has demonstrated analytical and commercial strategy foundation; ramp period likely 3–4 months with mentoring from Flix yield management team

gaps.gap: Transportation/Mobility/Hospitality domain experience: JD lists these sectors as 'ideally' preferred; candidate's experience is at Google Devices and Services
gaps.mitigation: Domain-agnostic commercial operations and pricing logic transfer well; Flix's travel/mobility context is straightforward to acquire; no hard blocker stated

gaps.gap: Confirmed SQL and BI tool proficiency: JD requires 'Advanced Excel' and lists Power BI or similar as 'strong plus'; candidate SQL and BI experience not documented in profile
gaps.mitigation: Excel expertise confirmed; BI tool adoption (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) is typical onboarding for analytics-minded operations leaders; SQL can be learned or delegated

gaps.gap: Direct market-level P&L or regional budget ownership unclear: role involves 'regional commercial strategy' for Central European portfolio; candidate background in global operations may not include direct market P&L accountability
gaps.mitigation: Cross-functional commercial steering and revenue target collaboration are demonstrated; formal P&L ownership can be clarified at interview; responsibility scope alignment critical for success

output.level_rationale: JD requires 2+ years in Revenue Management, Pricing, or Commercial Strategy; regional market portfolio ownership in Central Europe with cross-functional collaboration (Marketing, Sales, Network Planning, Product) suggests mid-level IC scope. Title "Manager" and market-level responsibility suggest L4; no clear managing-manager scope or P&L authority stated. Company stage/size unknown.
output.summary: Commercial Market Specialist at Flix is a mid-level operational and strategic role in pricing and revenue management for a Central European market portfolio. Candidate brings eight years of commercial operations leadership and cross-functional transformation experience at Google, with strong analytical and stakeholder-management foundations. Core gaps are direct revenue management / yield-pricing depth, transportation/mobility sector knowledge, and confirmed SQL/BI tool proficiency. Role-family fit is moderate (operations and commercial strategy align; pricing/revenue management requires ramp). Seniority appears appropriate (L4), though regional P&L ownership scope should be clarified. Stretch rating reflects strong commercial operations and transformation background offset by pricing/yield and sector domain gaps, all manageable for an engaged learner.
uncertainties: Company size and stage (Flix context): JD does not state whether Flix is early-stage, growth, or mature; affects interpretation of 'Manager' title and regional portfolio scope
Candidate's direct experience with transportation, mobility, or hospitality sectors
Depth of SQL and BI tool proficiency (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) beyond Excel
Whether candidate has held formal regional market P&L or revenue accountability
